name: Lancelot
description: Sir Lancelot, also known as Lancelot du Lac, is arguably the most famous knight of King Arthur's Round Table. Born the son of King Ban of Benwick and Queen Elaine, he was raised by the Lady of the Lake after his father's death. Renowned for his unparalleled chivalry, strength, and swordsmanship, Lancelot became Arthur's most trusted knight and champion. His prowess in battle was unmatched, and he was often called upon to defend the weak and uphold the ideals of Camelot. However, his tragic flaw was his illicit love affair with Queen Guinevere, which ultimately contributed to the downfall of Arthur's kingdom. This passionate but forbidden love created a deep conflict within Lancelot, torn between his loyalty to Arthur and his overwhelming desire for Guinevere. Despite his best intentions, his actions led to betrayals, battles, and the fracturing of the Round Table, forever tarnishing his legacy as the greatest knight of all time. He represents the complexity of human nature, where even the noblest of individuals can be undone by their own desires and weaknesses.
subtitle: The Greatest Knight, Torn by Love

keyMyths.title: Lancelot's Birth and Upbringing
keyMyths.description: Lancelot was the son of King Ban and Queen Elaine. After his father's death, he was raised by the Lady of the Lake, endowing him with magical abilities and exceptional training.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur by Thomas Malory

keyMyths.title: Lancelot's Love for Guinevere
keyMyths.description: Lancelot fell deeply in love with Queen Guinevere, leading to a secret and passionate affair. This forbidden love became a central conflict in the Arthurian legends, contributing to Camelot's downfall.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur by Thomas Malory

keyMyths.title: Lancelot's Healing of Sir Urre
keyMyths.description: Lancelot, through his purity and grace, was the only knight capable of healing the mortally wounded Sir Urre. This act demonstrated his exceptional chivalry and divine favor.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur by Thomas Malory

sources.author: Thomas Malory
sources.description: A compilation of Arthurian legends, focusing on Lancelot's role.
sources.text: A compilation of Arthurian legends, focusing on Lancelot's role.

sources.author: Chrétien de Troyes
sources.work: Lancelot, the Knight of the Cart
sources.description: An early Arthurian romance focusing on Lancelot's love for Guinevere.
sources.text: An early Arthurian romance focusing on Lancelot's love for Guinevere.
